metadata.intended_use: Citizen-facing welfare guidance recommendations and next-step referrals.
metadata.important_note: Programme rules, payment amounts, and availability can change. Final eligibility must be confirmed with the relevant authority.

programs.summary: Main government welfare cash-transfer pathway for low-income and vulnerable households.
programs.benefit_note: Payment bands and categories are set through the Welfare Benefits Board and gazette updates.
next_steps: Check beneficiary or application status through the Welfare Benefits Board.
Contact the Welfare Benefits Board hotline or visit your Divisional Secretariat for guidance.
Keep your NIC, proof of address, bank account details, and income information ready.

programs.summary: Government support pathway for eligible elderly citizens and low-income households with older dependents.
programs.benefit_note: Allowance details may vary by gazette and current payment cycle.
next_steps: Ask the Divisional Secretariat or Welfare Benefits Board about the current elderly support process.
Prepare identity documents for the elderly household member and income-related details.
documents: NIC of applicant and elderly household member
Any pension or support-status records if requested

programs.authority: National Secretariat for Persons with Disabilities
programs.summary: Support pathway for persons with disabilities, including a monthly living allowance and related assistance.
programs.benefit_note: Current NSPD pages indicate a monthly disability allowance and related assistance services.
next_steps: Meet the Social Service Officer at your local secretariat and ask for the disability allowance process.
Obtain or keep a government medical recommendation or disability certification ready.
Ask whether assistive-device, medical-aid, or self-employment support also applies.
documents: Medical report or government doctor recommendation
Completed application through local social services office

programs.authority: National Secretariat for Persons with Disabilities / Local Social Services
programs.summary: Allowance pathway for eligible low-income kidney patients.
programs.benefit_note: The exact allowance amount should be verified on the current official page when shown to users.
next_steps: Meet the Social Service Officer at the local secretariat to begin the application process.
Bring a government doctor's recommendation confirming kidney disease status.
Prepare income-related documents if requested.

programs.authority: National Disaster Relief Services Centre / Divisional Secretariat
programs.summary: Official disaster-relief pathway for affected households, including relief, camp support, and emergency assistance.
programs.benefit_note: Type and scale of assistance depend on disaster impact assessment and current relief guidance.
next_steps: Contact the Divisional Secretariat immediately after a disaster impact.
Ask about relief items, dry rations, medical treatment support, and housing-damage assistance.
Keep photos, damage details, and local verification records if available.
documents: Damage evidence such as photos if available
Any verification from local authorities if available

programs.summary: Public health pathway for citizens who need OPD, inpatient, or specialist care through government services.
programs.benefit_note: This is a universal service pathway rather than a targeted cash-transfer benefit.
next_steps: Visit the nearest government hospital or clinic for assessment.
Carry clinic books, prescriptions, and any existing medical records.
If long-term support is needed, ask about referral pathways through social services as well.
documents: Clinic book or medical records if available
Prescriptions or referral letters if available

programs.authority: School / Education Authorities / Local Welfare Offices
programs.summary: Referral pathway for low-income households with school-age children who may need school-related support.
programs.benefit_note: Availability can depend on school-level, local, or programme-specific support arrangements.
next_steps: Speak with the school principal, zonal education office, or local welfare office about current support schemes.
Ask whether school-meal, supplies, scholarship, or hardship support is available.

programs.summary: Community-level NGO referral for low-income households, village support, and emergency/community assistance.
programs.benefit_note: Availability may vary by district and local programme presence.
next_steps: Check whether Sarvodaya has an active district or village-level programme in your area.
Ask about food support, village development assistance, or emergency/community referrals.
documents: Any local referral or hardship information if available

programs.summary: NGO referral for elderly care, eye care, mobile clinics, and related elder support services.
programs.benefit_note: Useful as a targeted elder-support referral rather than a government welfare entitlement.
next_steps: Contact HelpAge Sri Lanka to ask about elder-support, eye-care, or mobile-clinic options.
If vision or mobility is a concern, ask specifically about eye hospital or medical outreach services.

programs.summary: Livelihood and microfinance referral for households seeking self-employment or small-business support.
programs.benefit_note: This is not a welfare cash grant; it is a finance/livelihood pathway.
next_steps: Check whether Sarvodaya Development Finance serves your area and whether a livelihood product is suitable.
Ask about microloans, agricultural finance, and financial-literacy support.
documents: Any existing self-employment details if applicable
